codeReview.summary: Targeted eviction and bounded cache plumbing are implemented, but channel-scoped eviction misses a real dependent cache key path: workspace bootstrap entries cached with `selected_channel_id = null` are not evicted even when they contain the mutated channel snapshot.

issues.description: `evict_channel` only evicts `WorkspaceBootstrap` keys where `selected_channel_id == Some(channel_id)`. However, the cache key is built from request params (`backend/src/handlers/channels.rs:69`), and `null` requests are valid; `load_workspace_bootstrap_data` then resolves `null` to the first channel (`backend/src/db/videos.rs:369-374`). Result: mutating that auto-selected channel can leave stale bootstrap payloads in cache until TTL expiry.

`evict_channel_does_not_affect_workspace_bootstrap_for_other_channels` asserts that `selected_channel_id: None` bootstrap entries must survive `evict_channel`. This test bakes in the stale-default-bootstrap behavior above, so the suite currently protects a regression-prone invalidation rule.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture notes are stale for backend cache behavior after this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:19 still says read cache uses global `clear()` on every mutation (14 call sites), but commit e04df31 replaces this with scoped eviction methods.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The distinction between workspace bootstrap cache key (`params.selected_channel_id`) and resolved selected channel (fallback to first channel) is not documented, yet it is critical for correct invalidation design.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/src/handlers/channels.rs:69 uses param-based key; backend/src/db/videos.rs:369-374 resolves `None` to `channels.first()` for payload selection.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust backend skill requires explicit RED proof (new tests fail first), but transcript/handoff evidence shows tests and implementation landed in one pass while still marked as followed procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/rust-backend-worker/SKILL.md:28-34 requires RED failure; handoff file line 68 says tests and implementation were added in one pass and suggests tightening this guidance.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking invalidation gap: channel mutations do not evict cached workspace bootstrap entries keyed with `selected_channel_id = null`, even though those entries may contain the mutated channel via fallback selection.
